title: Reclaimed Soda Crate Caddy
summary: A vintage soda-crate-style tote with a cut-out handle — for condiments, garden tools, or craft supplies. A one-board scrap build with a jigsaw handle.
description:
This is a small carry caddy styled after an old wooden soda crate: a boxy tote with a raised center divider that doubles as a handle. It's a one-afternoon scrap project — the whole thing comes out of a 1x8 and a bit of 1x6 — and it's a great use for reclaimed or pallet wood, where a little age and character actually improves the look.

The only shaped part is the handle board. Drill two 1" holes with a hole saw where the ends of the hand-hole go, then jigsaw out the waste between them to make a comfortable grip. Clip the top corners at 45° and round the top edge with the jigsaw for that classic crate silhouette. Everything else is straight cuts and butt joints.

Assembly is fast: attach the side pieces to the central handle board, then the remaining sides, then tack the bottom on. Glue plus 2" finish nails is all it needs — a brad nailer makes it go very quickly. The source entry didn't list tools, so this build infers them from the steps (a hole saw and jigsaw for the handle, a nailer for assembly). Leave the reclaimed wood as-is for patina, or clean it up and finish. It makes a charming gift filled with something.

tools.note: Drills the two 1" holes that form the ends of the hand-hole.
Cuts out the handle between the holes and rounds the top.
Soften edges; reclaimed wood can stay rustic.

materials.name: 1x8, 2 ft
materials.note: Handle/divider board — reclaimed or pallet wood is ideal.

materials.name: Finish
materials.note: Optional — leave reclaimed wood raw for patina.

cutList.note: Two 1" holes + jigsaw cut-out for the grip; top corners clipped 45° and rounded.

steps.title: Cut the handle board
steps.body: Cut the handle from the 1x8. Drill two 1" holes with a hole saw where the ends of the hand-hole will be, then jigsaw out the waste between them to make the grip. Clip the top corners at 45° off square and round the top edge with the jigsaw for the crate look.

steps.title: Attach the first sides to the handle
steps.body: Attach the side pieces to the handle board with glue and 2" finish nails — a nailer makes this go fast. The handle board is the center spine, so the sides register off it.

steps.title: Attach the remaining sides
steps.body: Attach the remaining side pieces the same way, glue and 2" finish nails, to build out the box on both sides of the handle.

steps.title: Attach the bottom and finish
steps.body: Tack the bottom on with finish nails and glue to close the caddy. Soften any sharp edges. Leave reclaimed wood raw for patina, or clean it up and finish.

images.alt: Rustic reclaimed-wood soda crate caddy with a cut-out handle
